title: Police at freshman student’s dorm room at Morgan State University following the shooting last night
body:
Theyre on campus. No warrant necessary

Edit: the fourth amendment doesn’t stop them from taking advantage of the fact that your on school grounds and that warrant, if possessed is serviced by the school. The fifth allows you not to speak people still found torture loopholes. I did not have this knowledge in college. Point is, if you dont know your rights, they will happily violate them because they legally can unless you win the attempt to out lawyer the university and then Good Lukc getting the classes you need….etc

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: My dad was an Aggie, and he survived WWII, came back to A&T, became a guest instructor in the math dept., went on to start his own business, married well, and raised their three kids to become successful. His name? Sgt. AR Steele, Mechanical Engineering at NC A& T , veteran of the Battle of the Bulge, and a good dad and role model. Not perfect, but a good example.
author: @LillianSteele-p7q

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: I have a master's degree in engineering, and I advise all young black males to seek other opportunities first before deciding to attend a four-year university. The accumulation of debt one has to take out to graduate from a four-year institution is a lot of times not worth it due to a variety of factors pertaining to the job market. $100,000+ in debt to start off at $50,000 a year with limited upward mobility seems to be a lot of graduate's situations and that's terrible.
author: @Moneyball23

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: They can't find good jobs after graduation so maybe some are realizing its truly a waste of their time. All they get stuck with is a huge student loan bill that will be nothing, but a problem to pay back.

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: I have a Bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering and working on my Master’s. I regret it. Noone told me how little job opportunities there would be and how little employers care about qualifications. Its who you know not what you know.

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: I have a suggestion for HBCUs in general, and Delaware State University in particular: instead of suspending students who are struggling academically, focus on providing more support to help them improve. When students are sent home, many end up working, and the chances of returning to college—especially when academic and financial help is limited—become very slim.
author: @tf5655

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: I was lucky when I attended an HBCU, Howard University in the early  1970's. I applied for the BEOG (Basic Educational Opportunity Grant), a federal student grant program in the 1970s that provided direct financial aid to low-income students to help pay for college. I didn't have to pay this money back or had any student debts. Created in 1972 and first awarded in the 1973-74 academic year, the program was renamed the Pell Grant in 1980 and served as the foundation for federal student aid. In the 1970s, the BEOG program's size grew, and eligibility was expanded to include middle-income students in 1978, although that expansion was later repealed. If it weren't for the BEOG Grant, I wouldn't have been able to attend college. I later earned an MBA at Georgetown University. Howard University Class of 1977 and a Georgetown University 1992 graduate.
author: @santurcedc

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: I work in higher ed and it quite honestly is just the expense of schooling. Debt is unattractive and millennials like myself encourage folks not to fall for the okie doke like we did. Address academic costs, room and board costs and make education truly AFFORDABLE and you’ll see your enrollment sky rocket. I love my HBCU, but if I could do it again in a different way financially, I would without question.
author: @elijahferebee8259

title: Black male enrollment decline: Morgan State and HBCUs push new programs to reverse the trend
body: This could be just the beginning. The enrollment cliff that's supposed to happen in 2026 because of the 2008 Great Recession could lower enrollment even more.
